title: Aunt Sue's Stories by Langston Hughes | Explanation in Hindi
description:
Langston Hughes wrote "Aunt Sue's Stories" in 1921 at just nineteen years old, capturing something that no classroom could easily teach — the living, breathing power of Black oral storytelling passed from a grandmother's lips to a child sitting in summer darkness. Far more than a simple poem about bedtime tales, it holds themes of cultural memory and Black resilience at its quiet, powerful core, making it essential for BA English Honours students studying the Harlem Renaissance. If you've ever wondered why this poem moves people so deeply, this Hindi explanation will finally make it click.
